business.headline: US drops fraud charges against Adani amid proposed $10 billion investment planThe move is being seen as the latest example of the Trump administration stepping away from high-profile prosecutions initiated during former President Joe Biden's administration.

India needs to triple its strategic petroleum reserves: Energy expert Anas AlhajjiIn an exclusive interview with India Today, energy expert Anas Alhajji spoke on the global oil crisis caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz due to the West Asia war and its impact on the economy and more

Bengaluru engineer, 25, has net worth of Rs 40 lakh. Here's how he built itNo inheritance, no family wealth and no shortcuts, yet a young Bengaluru engineer says he managed to build Rs 40 lakh in net worth surprisingly early. His story, shared on Reddit, is now catching attention for its simple money lessons.

Be remembered for Good: Seagram's 100 Pipers Travel Gear and India Today group are spotlighting stories that matterExploring the stories of individuals who are using their influence, work, and intent to create real-world impact.

India will keep buying Russian oil despite US waiver changes, says govt officialIndia said it will continue buying crude oil from Russia regardless of the US sanctions waiver position. The statement underlined New Delhi's focus on energy security and commercially viable supplies amid elevated oil prices.

How India's richest are investing differently, and what you can learn from itAlternative Investment Funds are structured differently. Category I and Category III AIFs, in particular, allow fund managers to run concentrated portfolios with fewer stocks, higher conviction, and deeper research.
Sensex recovers to end 77 points up, Nifty above 23,600; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex added 77.05 points to end at 75,315.04, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 6.45 points to close at 23,649.95.

Markets under pressure, rupee weakens. Are index funds a safer long-term bet?Sharp swings in equities, a weaker rupee and higher crude prices have pushed investors to reassess their strategy. Analysts say Nifty 50 index funds are drawing attention as a lower-cost long-term option, though volatility remains high.

What salary do you need to buy a home in Delhi-NCR now?A Rs 1 crore budget today may not fetch a decent home in many parts of Gurgaon or Delhi, while buyers earning even Rs 20 lakh annually are struggling to balance EMIs, savings, and lifestyle costs.

ITR filing for FY 2025–26 kicks off: Here's what you need to knowReady to file your ITR for FY 2025–26? The Income Tax Department has officially started this year's filing process by enabling ITR-1 and ITR-4 utilities. But what does this mean for taxpayers, and should you file early?

Why is it so difficult for NRIs to invest in India? Zerodha's Nithin Kamath explainsIndia wants more foreign capital, but could one of its biggest investor groups already be waiting at the door? Zerodha co-founder Nithin Kamath has raised questions over why NRIs still face so many hurdles while investing in India.

8th Pay Commission: When are revised salaries for govt employees likely to come?Speaking exclusively with IndiaToday.in, Dr Manjeet Singh Patel, National President of the All India NPS Employees Federation (AINPS), explained when central government employees could realistically expect revised salaries and pensions under the 8th Pay Commission.

Why has gold rally suddenly paused? Key reasons explainedJust days after gold prices surged sharply, the rally appears to have lost some steam. MCX gold saw only a small rise on Monday, signalling caution in the market. Here's a look at the key reasons why the yellow metal has suddenly slowed down.

Donald Trump made 3,700 stock trades in just 90 days. Wall Street is shockedFinancial disclosures linked to US President Donald Trump reveal more than 3,700 stock trades in just three months, triggering alarm on Wall Street over the scale, timing and potential conflicts involved.

Sensex down 400 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The benchmark BSE Sensex plunged 939.10 points, or 1.25%, to 74,298.89 at 9:37 am, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 284 points, or 1.20%, to 23,359.50.

ICICI Prudential Life shares down 7% today: Why the stock is falling sharplyICICI Prudential Life shares dropped Rs 39.70, or 7.41%, to Rs 495.90 on the National Stock Exchange of India as of 9:32 am, making it one of the top losers on the benchmark indices.

Sensex opens over 800 points lower, Nifty below 23,400; Tata Steel down 4%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 807.71 points to 74,430.28 at 9:18 am, while the Nifty 50 slipped 249.75 points to 23,393.75. Most of the broader market indices also slipped.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Markets are set for a weak open after a drone attack on a UAE nuclear plant pushed oil prices higher. Rising crude, a record-low rupee and mixed institutional flows are keeping sentiment cautious.

Will petrol and diesel prices rise again after Russian oil waiver ends?India may face fresh pressure on petrol and diesel prices after the US let a waiver on Russian oil purchases expire. With crude above USD 100 and West Asia tensions high, refiners and consumers could come under added strain.

Gautam Adani launches Rs 150 crore rural eye care initiative in BiharTogether, the Adani Centre for Eye (ACE) and Adani Training in Ophthalmic Medicine (ATOM) will create an ecosystem that will be capable of performing 3.3 lakh eye surgeries annually and training 1,000 eye care professionals every year.

entertainment.headline: Ram Charan: Peddi with Janhvi Kapoor honours Chiranjeevi-Sridevi legacy Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor highlighted the emotional significance of their pairing in Peddi. Their collaboration evokes the cherished cinematic legacy of their parents, Chiranjeevi and Sridevi.

Prashanth Neel to gift 4.5 minute glimpse of Jr NTR's film ahead of his birthday The makers of Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel's film will unveil its first glimpse late on May 19. Its reported 4-minute,35-second runtime and the possibility Dragon title has heightened fan curiosity.

Sivakarthikeyan's Seyon begins with puja, makers share new intense poster Sivakarthikeyan has started work on Seyon, directed by Sivakumar Murugesan and backed by Raaj Kamal Films International. The film has drawn attention for its rural action setting, mythological undertones, and echoes of Kamal Haasan's Virumaandi.

Thanks to Sultan, Dangal: Ram Charan credits Salman, Aamir for shaping Peddi At the Peddi trailer launch, Ram Charan said Salman Khan and Aamir Khan paved the way for sports dramas like his film. He credited films like Sultan and Dangal for shaping his upcoming Telugu film.

Vir Das responds to selective criticism charge: Did Congress jokes for 7 years Vir Das hit back after an X user accused him of targeting only the BJP-led Centre. The exchange renewed focus on his political satire and the backlash it often attracts.

GOAT maker Venkat Prabhu gifts CM Vijay 'TN07 CM 2026' number plate from film Director Venkat Prabhu presented Chief Minister Vijay with the 'TN07 CM 2026' number plate from GOAT film. The movie released months after Vijay launched Tamilaga Vetri Kazhagam (TVK) in February 2024.

Cannes gets Marathi touch as Ashok Saraf, 78, walks red carpet with wife Nivedita Ashok Saraf, 78, attended the Cannes Film Festival with wife Nivedita as part of a Marathi delegation. The actor couple's appearance at the Indian Pavilion and on the red carpet put Marathi cinema and Maharashtrian culture in global view.

Peddi trailer X reaction: Internet divided over Ram Charan, Janhvi Kapoor's film The trailer of the upcoming movie Peddi, starring Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor, has drawn mixed reactions on X. It is slated to release in theatres on June 4.

Harsh Chhaya reveals he went on multiple dates after split with Shefali Shah Harsh Chhaya has spoken again about his divorce from Shefali Shah and how he coped after their separation. He said divorce should not be treated as a taboo and described it as a personal decision.

Peddi trailer: Ram Charan plays sports hero, faces bigger fight beyond the field The trailer of Peddi introduces Ram Charan as a village sports hero who stands up for his people. Also starring Janhvi Kapoor and directed by Buchi Babu Sana, the sports action drama will hit theatres on June 4.

Bhumi Pednekar moved to tears after meeting Dalai Lama Actor Bhumi Pednekar shared an emotional note after meeting the Dalai Lama at a monastery. She said the encounter left her in tears and gave her a lasting sense of peace, grace and compassion.

Once a cheater, always a cheater? Sara Ali Khan, Ayushmann Khurrana differ Actors Sara Ali Khan and Ayushmann Khurrana clashed over whether a cheater can change. The duo recently appeared in the film Pati, Patni Aur Wo Do, which was released in theatres on May 15.

Happy that Vijay sir won, he's still the same person: Actor Sivakarthikeyan Actor Sivakarthikeyan said he was happy to see Vijay become Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and recalled speaking to him after his victory. He said Vijay sounded calm and expressed confidence that he would handle the new role well.

Shiva Rajkumar praises Vijay's rise, congratulates him on becoming CM Shiva Rajkumar congratulated Vijay on becoming Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and wished him strength to deliver good governance during the promotion of his upcoming film Bail.

Suriya's Karuppu avatar sparks Stranger Things buzz, draws Vecna comparisons Suriya's Karuppu looks have triggered online comparisons with Stranger Things after theatre clips went viral. The chatter has centred on his Karuppasamy avatar, which fans believed resembled Vecna in the Netflix series.

CM Vijay remembers producer K Rajan, says Tamil cinema will remember his legacy Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay expressed grief over producer K Rajan's death and paid tribute to his career. The veteran filmmaker's death has shaken the Tamil film industry and prompted an investigation.

Sreeleela focussed on NEET-PG June exams: Mother dismisses Tilak Verma dating buzz Sreeleela's mother and team have denied rumours linking the actor with cricketer Tilak Varma. They said the two have never met, as she remains focused on films and her NEET-PG preparation.

Karthik Subbaraj teams up with Ilaiyaraaja for milestone 10th film, shares video Director Karthik Subbaraj announced his first collaboration with Ilaiyaraaja for his 10th film, with the legendary composer also marking his 1540th project in a career spanning 50 years.

Welcome To The Jungle title track: OG vibes meet jungle chaos in Akshay's film Welcome To The Jungle's title track brings back the comic energy of the Welcome franchise with a nostalgic callback and a massive ensemble cast. The film is slated to release in theatres on June 26.

general news.headline: Trump puts planned Iran attack on hold after Qatar, Saudi, UAE leaders' requestsQatar, Saudi Arabia and the UAE have held differing positions on Iran. However, if Trump is right, Gulf countries now appear united in attempting to prevent a direct military confrontation between Washington and Tehran that could destabilise the entire region.

Twisha Sharma death case: Chilling chats, absconding husband, powerful in-lawsTwisha Sharma was found hanging at her matrimonial home in Bhopal, prompting a dowry death case against her husband and mother-in-law. Her family's allegations, chat records and the bail proceedings have made the investigation legally and politically sensitive.

US drops fraud charges against Adani amid proposed $10 billion investment planThe move is being seen as the latest example of the Trump administration stepping away from high-profile prosecutions initiated during former President Joe Biden's administration.

Dear NTA, at least say sorry: A student writes for lakhs of NEET aspirantsIn a sharply worded letter to the National Testing Agency, an 18-year-old NEET 2026 aspirant recounts months of sacrifice and the shock of a re-NEET after reports of a paper leak, reflecting the anger and broken trust many candidates feel.

Police crackdown on namaz protesters, Bengal CM sends big Park Circus messageProtesters clashed with police at Kolkata's Park Circus on Sunday, which left at least 10 cops injured. West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari reached Park Circus on Monday and warned people protesting over curbs on namaz on roads and anti-encroachment drives of strict action. Around 40 people have been arrested over the violence.

Elon Musk loses lawsuit against Sam Altman's OpenAIJurors in federal court in Oakland unanimously found that Elon Musk had filed the lawsuit too late. They rejected his claims that OpenAI had abandoned its original mission of developing artificial intelligence for the benefit of humanity rather than corporate profit.

Bengal ends allowances for imams, purohits, says govt's focus is on educationThe Bengal government's latest cabinet decisions have triggered sharp political debate, with religious stipends scrapped, women-focused welfare expanded and Suvendu Adhikari pushing a governance model centred on "no appeasement" and direct outreach.

Tamil Nadu CM Vijay orders Amma canteen revamp after food quality complaintsTamil Nadu CM Vijay has ordered the renovation and modernisation of Amma Unavagams across the state. The move follows reports about the quality and taste of food served at the subsidised canteens.

Woman dies after elephant rams another jumbo, loses balance and falls on herA tourist from Tamil Nadu died after two captive elephants were seen fighting at Dubare Elephant Camp in Kodagu. The accident has prompted an inquiry and renewed concerns over visitor safety during close-contact activities.

CCTV shows Twisha Sharma heading to roof, husband carrying her body an hour laterExclusive CCTV footage, claims of psychiatric treatment, allegations of dowry harassment and a missing husband have turned Twisha Sharma's death into one of Madhya Pradesh's most explosive and emotionally charged investigations in recent weeks.

All will be forgiven: US orchestrated Imran Khan's ouster, leaked cable revealsA leaked Pakistani diplomatic cable has reignited controversy over the ouster of former Prime Minister Imran Khan, with investigative outlet Drop Site publishing the original document at the centre of the cricketer-turned-politician's claims of US-backed regime change.

Twisha took abortion pill: Mother-in-law's fresh claim as death mystery deepensRetired judge Giribala Singh said Twisha Sharma took a Medical Termination of Pregnancy pill and informed her husband by phone. The claim has added a new layer to the mystery surrounding the 33-year-old's death.

Sreeleela focussed on NEET-PG June exams: Mother dismisses Tilak Verma dating buzzSreeleela's mother and team have denied rumours linking the actor with cricketer Tilak Varma. They said the two have never met, as she remains focused on films and her NEET-PG preparation.

First look of India's debut bullet train between Mumbai, Ahmedabad unveiledA picture of the high-speed train set has been installed at Gate Number 4 of the Ministry of Railways building. The display showcases the design of the train.

India is now Naxal-free: Amit Shah declares end of insurgency in ChhattisgarhAmit Shah said in Bastar that India is now free of Naxalism after decades of insurgency. He linked the claim to security forces' sacrifices and outlined rehabilitation and development plans.

Do Indians listen to PM Modi? Lakshadweep tourism numbers tell a storyRTI data accessed by India Today shows that tourist arrivals in Lakshadweep surged from just 3,875 in 2020 to a record 68,328 in 2024. The sharpest jump came after PM Modi's January 2024 visit to the islands.

Candidate's twerks and Only Fans-like campaign shocks Democrats, votersMichigan Democratic congressional candidate Shelby Campbell is facing backlash after several explicit dances and twerking videos from her campaign went viral online ahead of the August 2026 election. Campbell is facing massive backlash over her unconventional social media election strategy and a series of past controversies tied to her campaign.

No relief from heat: Intense heatwave to dominate India on TuesdayWhile heat continues to sweep India, relief is in the fact that the monsoon season has arrived earlier than its typical schedule and will soon reach northern regions.

India gets breather as US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver by 30 daysIndia earlier signalled that it would continue purchasing Russian crude irrespective of the status of the US sanctions waiver, underlining that energy security and commercial considerations remain its priority.

Indian schools don't teach how to be happy: IAS officer with IIT, IIM degrees shares postIAS officer Divya Mittal's post on X about what elite education did not teach her triggered a wide debate. The exchange widened into questions over emotional health, life skills, exam culture and opportunity.

Why Vijay's alcohol double shot will resonate with his fansIn Tamil Nadu, alcohol is linked to more than just evening cheers. It spills into cinema, masculinity, and politics. CM C Joseph Vijay is shutting down TASMAC booze shops and enforcing a minimum age for alcohol access. This is why Vijay is confident that the moves won't backfire.

Cockroach Janta Party debuts, admits two TMC MPs within daysThe latest political gimmick or party in India, whichever way you see it, is the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP). Coming right after Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant's cockroach remarks, the CJP is on a membership drive. Within two days, it has admitted two TMC MPs, Mahua Moitra and Kirti Azad.

Why Vijay stayed away from Kerala swearing-in despite Rahul camaraderieCongress brought together senior leaders for VD Satheesan's swearing-in in Kerala, but Vijay stayed away. His absence drew attention over whether it reflected logistics, political caution or both.

8th Pay Commission: When are revised salaries for govt employees likely to come?Speaking exclusively with IndiaToday.in, Dr Manjeet Singh Patel, National President of the All India NPS Employees Federation (AINPS), explained when central government employees could realistically expect revised salaries and pensions under the 8th Pay Commission.

sports.headline: Whistle, poda: Ishan Kishan's send-off to CSK fans at Chepauk goes viral Jeered throughout his innings against Chennai Super Kings, Ishan Kishan decided to give the CSK fans a send-off after guiding SRH to a win on Monday. Kishan's gesture towards the fans went viral after the game.

Sun rises in Chepauk as Hyderabad grit it out to seal a playoff berth IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad sealed their place in the IPL 2026 playoffs with a composed five-wicket win over Chennai Super Kings at Chepauk. While the night carried emotional weight around the possible final home chapter of MS Dhoni, Ishan Kishan and Pat Cummins ensured the spotlight ultimately belonged to their playoff-clinching performance.

Samson's Dhoni-esque stumping rattles Heinrich Klaasen. SRH batter abuses keeper Sanju Samson's lightning-fast stumping saw the back of Heinrich Klaasen in CSK vs SRH. Tempers flared as Samson stared down the batter, who slipped in some words while leaving the ground.

Who is Karman Kaur Thandi? India's former No.1 set for French Open debut Karman Kaur Thandi enters the 2026 French Open qualifiers using a protected ranking, with the former India No.1 set to face France's Harmony Tan in her opening-round clash. The 27-year-old, once ranked No. 196, is aiming for her first-ever Grand Slam main-draw appearance.

Asian Games: 4-time Olympian Deepika Kumari dropped from archery team India has finalised its archery team for the Asian Games. After a gruelling 3-day trials in Sonipat, Haryana, 4-time Olympian Deepika Kumari has been left out of the squad.

15 runs in 21 balls, 0 boundaries: CSK fans snap at Ruturaj Gaikwad Chennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad played an absolute howler in their last home game of the season. Gaikwad lasted 21 balls where he had a strike-rate of 71.

Cirstea breaks new ground in farewell season, becomes oldest top 20 debutant Sorana Cirstea's farewell season saw a historic surge as she reached the Italian Open semi-finals and became the oldest debutant in the WTA Top 20 at 36. Her run included a win over world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka before ending with a 6–4, 6–3 loss to Coco Gauff.

Urvil Patel pays tribute to MS Dhoni at Chepauk, helicopters Cummins out of ground IPL 2026: Urvil Patel uncorked a Dhoni-style helicopter shot off Pat Cummins in CSK's last home game of the season. MS Dhoni was again deemed unfit for the game against Sunrisers Hyderabad on Monday, May 17.

After Mirpur brainfade, Pakistan double down, confident of chasing 437 in Sylhet BAN vs PAK, 2nd Test: Pakistan are confident of chasing 437 in the Sylhet Test, with bowling coach Umar Gul insisting the batting unit has the firepower and conditions are favourable on the second and third days.

MS Dhoni misses CSK's last home game, but retirement rumours dashed again IPL 2026: MS Dhoni travelled with Chennai Super Kings but stayed unavailable for the must-win home game against Sunrisers Hyderabad. His fitness issues have kept Chepauk waiting again and left only a faint chance of a return later.

Complete disaster: Ex-India head coach's slams Indian football administration Manolo Marquez has again criticised the way Indian football is being run as FC Goa prepare to face Kerala Blasters. With the ISL calendar unresolved and contracts nearing expiry, he says he cannot see a clear future for himself in India.

CSK vs SRH Highlights: Hyderabad qualify for play-offs, carry Gujarat along with themChennai (CSK) vs Hyderabad (SRH) Live Score, IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad have qualified for the playoffs after beating Chennai Super Kings by five wickets on Monday. With SRH's win, the Gujarat Titans also secured their berth in the top four.

Mushfiqur Rahim century leaves Pakistan with a mountain to climb in Sylhet TestBAN vs PAK, 2nd Test Day 3: Bangladesh dominated Day 3 of the Sylhet Test, setting Pakistan a massive target of 437 runs after Mushfiqur Rahim's brilliant 137 and Litton Das's fluent 69 in the second innings.

Arshdeep is fully fit: Punjab Kings contradict themselves on pacer's fitnessIPL 2026: Punjab Kings have found themselves under scrutiny once again after claiming that Arshdeep Singh is fully fit, contrary to media 'reports'. This comes just 48 hours after their own physio said the fast bowler had been playing with a niggle midway through the season.

India squad for Afghanistan series: Bumrah's workload, Rohit's fitness in focusIndia's squad selection for next month's one-off Test and ODI series against Afghanistan is set to revolve around the fitness of Rohit Sharma and the workload management of Jasprit Bumrah, with selectors also expected to discuss fresh faces like Prince Yadav and Aquib Nabi ahead of a crucial home season.

Is Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India call-up too early? Ex-IND player raises concernIPL 2026: Mohammad Kaif has sparked debate over an aspect of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's game despite the teenager's sensational IPL form. The remarks come even as the Rajasthan Royals youngster continues to earn massive praise for his record-breaking batting exploits.

Salah must be dropped from Liverpool farewell game for disrespecting Slot: RooneyWayne Rooney has launched fierce criticism of Mohamed Salah, accusing the Liverpool forward of being "selfish" and "disrespectful" towards manager Arne Slot after his latest public remarks on the club's style of play.

BCCI not a public authority under RTI Act: Central Information CommissionThe Central Information Commission (CIC) on Monday ruled that the BCCI is not a "public authority" under the RTI Act, saying the cricket board is neither owned, controlled nor substantially financed by the government.

Dhoni's return not on for IPL 2026? Ashwin's post sends CSK fans in panic modeRavichandran Ashwin's cryptic post has cast fresh doubt over MS Dhoni's return for Chennai Super Kings' home game against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The message has stirred anxiety among fans as Chennai juggle fitness concerns, team balance and playoff pressure.

MS Dhoni comeback talk dismissed by Gavaskar before must-win SRH gameFormer India captain Sunil Gavaskar said MS Dhoni is unlikely to return for Chennai Super Kings against Sunrisers Hyderabad, insisting that CSK's playoff push and Dhoni's incomplete recovery must take priority over sentiment.

Neymar slams 'nonsense' criticism: I have worked hard for FIFA World CupBrazil's record scorer Neymar said he had to endure "nonsense" criticism as he worked his way back from serious injuries and is satisfied he has done all he can to prove he is fully fit and deserves a place in Carlo Ancelotti's World Cup squad.

Punjab Kings co-owner, Mohit Burman open to owning a WPL franchisePunjab Kings co-owner Mohit Burman says the franchise would love to own a WPL team in future, backing the rapid rise of women's cricket despite the BCCI ruling out immediate expansion.

Vaughan hits out at ECB over Archer's extended IPL stay before Lord's TestFormer England captain Michael Vaughan has questioned the ECB's handling of Jofra Archer's availability ahead of England's first Test against New Zealand, saying central contracts must take precedence over IPL commitments.

KKR did not support me: Bangladesh's Litton Das makes bold claim on IPL stintBangladesh star Litton Das has criticised Kolkata Knight Riders over a lack of communication and support during his brief IPL stint, saying he was informed of his debut only hours before the match.

RR fell 20 runs short after Starc's game-changing over, admits RathourRajasthan Royals assistant coach Vikram Rathour admitted that Mitchell Starc's decisive 15th over turned the match in Delhi Capitals' favour, conceding that RR fell at least 20 runs short and paid for poor batting decisions in their five-wicket defeat.

Preity Zinta's intense chat with Shreyas Iyer goes viral as PBKS lose six in a rowPreity Zinta spoke at length with Shreyas Iyer after Punjab Kings lost to Royal Challengers Bengaluru in Dharamsala. The exchange drew attention as Punjab slumped to a sixth straight defeat and RCB sealed a playoff place.

Who is Aaron Rai? Indian-origin golfer ends Britain's 100-year PGA title waitAaron Rai, an Indian-origin golfer, surged past Alex Smalley and Jon Rahm to win the PGA Championship, becoming the first Englishman in 107 years to lift the title.

Virat Kohli deserves Glenn Phillips-level hype for athleticism, say fans after viral videoLivewire at 37, Virat Kohli left fans in awe during RCB's win over PBKS in Dharamshala. A viral clip of his electric fielding sparked massive social media reactions, with fans hailing the icon as the greatest athlete India has ever produced.

CSK vs SRH: Dhoni's potential farewell masks playoff drama in ChennaiChennai Super Kings face SunRisers Hyderabad in their final home game of the season. While a playoff spot might be at stake, the entire attention is set to be on the possibility that MS Dhoni might announce his retirement after the game.

Kotla feels like an away ground: Badani's brutal verdict on DC's home disadvantageDelhi Capitals coach Hemang Badani delivered a blunt assessment of his side's home struggles despite DC's crucial five-wicket win, admitting the Arun Jaitley Stadium no longer feels like a home venue after the team managed just two wins in seven matches in Delhi this IPL season.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
